entry.title: Washington Post: 26 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • E.U. agrees to watered-down gas-rationing plan after early resistance
  • Myanmar junta defiantly defends executions as U.S. presses China to do more
  • Russia says it’ll withdraw from International Space Station after 2024
  • After Russian port strike, Ukraine grain deal hangs in the balance
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: E.U. agrees gas rationing plan; Russia to pull out of International Space Station
  • Droughts and more wildfires may be next for Britain after record heat wave
  • Brittney Griner prepares to testify this week in Russian trial
  • Czech police seized a high-powered Ferrari. Now it’s in their fleet.
  • A Chinese artist fights pollution with rock music
  • U.K. parents lose battle to continue life support for 12-year-old in coma
  • Italy is on its way to being run by ‘post-fascists’
  • Other times popes have apologized for the sins of the Catholic Church
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Kyiv looks to retake occupied southern region; Russia cuts gas flows to Europe
  • The law professor who set out to dismantle Tunisia’s democracy
  • Myanmar junta executes four pro-democracy activists
  • Pope apologizes for ‘evil committed by so many Christians’ in Canada’s residential schools
  • What to know about Canada’s residential schools and the unmarked graves found nearby
  • Deadly shootings in Whistler and Langley rock British Columbia
  • UN: Violence rising in rural Colombia despite peace deal
  • Doctors group: Sudan forces kill at least 1 during protests
  • Iraq court overturns verdict against Briton, orders release
  • 5 killed, 50 injured in anti-UN protests in Congo's east
  • New Russian airstrikes target Black Sea regions of Ukraine
  • Saudi crown prince: First EU visit since Khashoggi killing
  • Macron pledges support to boost food production in Africa
  • National Guard member sought out extremists, planned attack on police
  • Uvalde school board calls on Texas to raise minimum age for assault weapons
  • Third set of human remains recovered at shrinking Lake Mead, park says
  • GOP lawmaker opposed same-sex marriage, then went to gay son’s wedding
  • As Sandy Hook defamation trial begins, Alex Jones might be absent
  • Med students walk out mid-ceremony to protest speaker’s antiabortion views
  • Preacher and his wife robbed of $1 million in jewelry during sermon
  • A town didn’t have a veterans memorial. So this teen designed and built one.
  • Post Politics Now: Trump returns to Washington; Biden on cusp of some wins
  • Biden administration weighs declaring monkeypox a health emergency
  • Biden poised for big wins in Congress
  • Post Politics Now: Biden’s covid symptoms ‘almost completely resolved,’ doctor says
  • Arizona fake-electors subpoenas show breadth of DOJ Jan. 6 probe
  • Biden moves to reinstate health protections for LGBTQ Americans
  • Biden slams Trump for watching Jan. 6 riot as police faced ‘medieval hell’
  • China targeted the Fed for more than a decade, Senate report finds
  • The changing shape of inflation
  • You should change these LinkedIn privacy settings now
  • Thanks to technology, you can now own the rights to an iconic Biggie rap
  • Nature preserve is pleasant surprise for some Broadlands, Va., buyers
  • How to create a low-maintenance water feature on your property
  • Cooling housing market brings relief to buyers
  • Perishing Casket Sales Aren’t a Death Sentence
  • Sneaky Shrinkflation Is Driving People Crazy
  • Crypto Breaks the Rules. That’s the Point.
  • Bond Investors Need to Liquidate China Evergrande Now
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why Blackouts Are Still Crippling South Africa
  • How Italy’s Political Drama Forced an Election
  • How Elon Musk Sparked a Race to Send Satellites into ‘LEO’
  • Democratic meddling in GOP primaries has reached a new level of absurd
  • We must contain monkeypox before it becomes a broader threat
  • If Democrats are losing the working class, it’s not because of the left
  • Democrats could pass a huge achievement — if they rally behind this measure
  • Without pensions, future retirees face financial trouble. Where’s Washington?
  • The Trump administration’s assault on the census must not happen again
  • Clinics where abortion is legal are at a breaking point. They need help.
  • 5 reasons Democrats could defy history in the midterms
  • The brutal end of German illusions about energy from Russia
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 26 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 26 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-26 19:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tim Winton tackles an unsettling new project, 40 years after his acclaimed debut
  • The bowler who stands between Australia and gold in Birmingham
  • ASX set to fall as grim Walmart warning hits Wall Street
  • Can online retailer Kogan relive its COVID glory days?
  • Insecure work in Australian higher education must end now
  • Desperately short of qualified staff, childcare centres ask to bend the rules
  • Australia urged to join New Zealand in tobacco ‘endgame’
  • ‘Risky investment’? What a deal for Dusty could look like
  • Should I have to wear a bra in the office if I don’t want to?
  • Our politics is suddenly boring, and that’s worth celebrating
  • Two-thirds of Australians haven’t been to the dentist in two years
  • Dusty just wanted to help calm an argument. Her act of kindness proved fatal
  • Inflation is not a big problem, so don’t hit it with a sledgehammer
  • Labor women’s caucus leader vows to push for equal, affordable access to abortion
  • Rugby needs fixing but meaningful change will have to wait
  • Tree-changers count savings, lifestyle gains
  • City slickers use COVID for sea and tree change
  • Building inspectors pressured to avoid finding faults
  • Tabcorp’s head of wagering and media resigns

  • My return trip from Bali augurs badly for our biosecurity
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘A wonderful time’: New Zealand politician, on a Hawaiian holiday, tells Kiwis he’s hard at work
  • French rivals tied up in knots over dress code for MPs
  • High ticket sales defy low-profile start to accidental Commonwealth Games
  • Truss campaign takes aim at Sunak’s wealth as Tory candidates clash in debate

  • Australia urged to ride out waves of disruption over next 20 years
  • Stinging plant offers insights into preventing pain
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Wednesday, July 27
  • Former Brisbane health service chief in court on nepotism charges
  • Pinkenba quarantine centre’s opening date and purpose still up in the air
  • Qld police officer tells assault victim ‘focus on being a good mother’, inquiry told
  • Power giants want new projects prioritised as coal is phased out
  • Westpac to offer 10-minute mortgage as it eyes refinancing surge
  • Pilot, Juniper healthcare brands to cut staff after investor pulls loan deal
  • Apartment buyers still need protection against building defects
  • Binge shopping: Rising retail tide lifts the good ship Myer
  • Telstra signs multi-year Microsoft deal to beef up digital arsenal
  • Developer Caydon collapses, threatening historic Nylex site project
  • Alarm bells are ringing as China’s property cancer spreads
  • Surprise sales surge at Myer tickles investors
  • APRA chair Wayne Byres to step down
  • The Wrap: ASX hits six-week high, investors eye incoming CPI data
  • Gordon Cairns to exit as Woolies chair after seven years
  • Elon Musk has turned into a liability for Tesla
  • Inside the billionaire family plot to oust Volkswagen’s CEO
  • Farewell to shiraz and wagyu: 155,000 borrowers to feel intense pain
  • Pioneering ‘champagne comedy’ stands the test of time – mostly
  • Melbourne Writers Festival returns with a bang after two-year absence
  • Neighbours top 10 moments: The scenes that defined Ramsay Street
  • Kylie Jenner’s tweet wiped $1.3b from Snapchat. Now she’s after Instagram
  • Facebook whistleblower Frances Haugen headlines Festival of Dangerous Ideas
  • The Sydney teen Irish dance sensation whose leaps into the Eire defy gravity
  • True story or unreliable source? Film about autism skips controversy
  • Her music, a fragment of her online self, was stolen by right-wing media
  • Larry Sanders is the best new comedy on TV, even after 30 years
  • How Saddam Hussein saved The Late Show, and helped shape Aussie comedy
  • Goodfellas, Law & Order actor Paul Sorvino dies at 83
  • Wet weather delays fire season preparation as rain set to continue to for months
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 26 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 26 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 26 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-26 18:35PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee, top judge and Olympic medallists among 890 named on city’s honours list
  • Hong Kong Book Fair 2022 closes with total of 850,000 visitors, a slight increase from last year’s event
  • Former CEO of China Everbright’s financial arm under investigation by Communist Party’s anti-corruption body
  • Hong Kong’s Exchange Fund to report ‘significant’ first-half loss as ‘perfect storm’ sends all assets into free fall, HKMA chief warns
  • Exclusive clubs took millions of Hong Kong dollars in wage subsidies designed to help cash-strapped firms during Covid fifth wave
  • Hong Kong court sentences 67-year-old man to life imprisonment for murdering wife after groceries dispute
  • Hong Kong and Shenzhen exchange operators join hands to promote Greater Bay Area as economic powerhouse, green finance hub
  • Hong Kong man who killed cancer-stricken wife pleads guilty to reduced charge of manslaughter
  • Hong Kong’s iconic The Peak Lookout restaurant has its rent slashed in half as pandemic wipes out international tourism
  • Hong Kong protests: magistrate committed ‘serious error’ by sending man guilty of assaulting officer to psychiatric centre, High Court judges find
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts call for doubling of antiviral distribution rate for elderly as city logs 4,276 cases
  • Hong Kong police officer draws gun to arrest knife-wielding man, 77, suspected of robbing shop for pack of cigarettes
  • Standard Chartered, HSBC expected to report weaker results as choppy markets, China’s economic slowdown weigh on outlook
  • Hong Kong motorcyclist, 59, dies after head-on collision with double-decker bus
  • Most Hong Kong pupils thrilled to get secondary school of choice, but for some, dismay and rush to find alternatives
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief speaks to the Post
  • Decouple Hong Kong’s currency peg to the US dollar? ‘Absolutely not,’ says finance chief Paul Chan
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief warns annual economic growth forecast could be cut, admits ‘most critical constraint’ has been border closure and pledges government is doing its all to ease rules
  • Soaring construction costs may discourage Hong Kong’s developers from bidding aggressively for land
  • Hong Kong must reopen its international border, to fulfil its destiny as China’s global city
  • George Gao Fu, head of China’s CDC who helped lead coronavirus pandemic response, stepping down
  • China rejects criticism that its zero-Covid policies violate the rights of US diplomats
  • Taiwan visit by Nancy Pelosi risks sparking US-PLA conflict, army watchers warn
  • Widodo’s ‘balancing act’ meeting Xi in Beijing ahead of G20
  • China’s top chip maker SMIC may have achieved tech breakthrough, experts say
  • Chinese human rights lawyer Chang Weiping in closed-door trial as family barred from county
  • China-Indonesia trade: how important is it, and what are the main exports?
  • How artefacts are being protected at the Sanxingdui Ruins in China
  • China, Indonesia vow stronger ties as Xi Jinping and Joko Widodo hold ‘kind and friendly’ talks
  • China’s ‘worse-than-anticipated’ economic slowdown at core of IMF cut to 2022 global growth forecast
  • Chinese tech hub Shenzhen rolls out ‘targeted measures’ to reduce industrial disruptions, contain fresh Covid-19 outbreak
  • China may increase cultural imports as part of 28-point plan to boost its soft power overseas
  • Digital sentries were sent to curb Covid-19. Will they still be keeping watch when the pandemic is over?
  • Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen observes live-fire drill from warship
  • China’s searing heat wave strains power grid as demand for air conditioning soars past last year’s record
  • China’s top basketball league slaps streaming video giant Bilibili with copyright lawsuit, US$60.12 million in compensation
  • China’s new mega tunnel will send water from the Three Gorges Dam to Beijing
  • Monkeypox in mainland China ‘only a matter of time’, says top scientist
  • Ant Group executives exit Alibaba partnership as fintech unit seeks regulatory greenlight
  • China tariffs see Australia’s wine exports suffer ‘not unexpected’ decline
  • Treasures shed light on ancient Chinese kingdom, but mysteries remain
  • China’s fast-fashion juggernaut Shein is seeing private bids US$30 billion below April valuation
  • China enforces regular government meetings to coordinate Big Tech policies as crackdown eases
  • More violence in Myanmar feared as junta’s executions send ‘ruthless’ message to Asean
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Najib Razak hires new lawyer, seeks postponement of final appeal hearing
  • US readies high-level delegation to visit Solomon Islands after China security pact
  • Thai film poster featuring Cambodia’s lamduan flower sparks social media dispute
  • Japan insurers start selling same-day heatstroke cover as temperatures soar
  • As India looks to Namibia for cheetahs, will it be a ‘waste of taxpayers’ money’?
  • Singapore’s Temasek bets big on food as world’s supply falters amid war, climate crisis
  • US fighter jets staged ‘largest-ever’ show of force against China, Japanese government sources say
  • Fashion brands from Zara to H&M urged to make ‘responsible exit’ from Myanmar
  • US calls on China to escalate pressure on Myanmar after junta’s execution of 4 jailed activists
  • China-Australia coal trade has ‘huge potential’, but is demand still there as Beijing mulls lifting ban?
  • Pet lovers upset after Polish institute classifies cats as ‘invasive alien species’
  • Russia to leave International Space Station after 2024 and build its own orbital outpost
  • European Union seals deal on reducing gas use as Russia squeezes supply
  • Germany’s Lufthansa cancels more than 1,000 flights amid ground staff strike for higher pay
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky replaces commander as emboldened Russia digs in
  • Mystery hepatitis outbreak in children not linked to Covid-19: studies
  • British PM finalists challenge each other on China in televised debate
  • Ukraine prosecutors push to try alleged war crimes as fighting rages
  • David Trimble, Northern Ireland’s Nobel peacemaker, dead at 77
  • UK to host 2023 Eurovision song contest as war-torn Ukraine bows out
  • Kaisa takes baby steps towards repaying US$18 billion of borrowings, aims to publish overdue 2021 accounts by October
  • As China’s economic challenges grow, will investors lose confidence?
  • How Hong Kong mall owners can stay abreast of China’s personal data protection law
  • Intel to produce chips for Taiwan’s MediaTek in big foundry push, an industry dominated by TSMC
  • Alibaba soars with HKEX with primary listing bid in Hong Kong while property developers extend rally on bailout speculation
  • Alibaba seeks Hong Kong primary listing for Stock Connect access, ‘wider and more diversified investor base’
  • Kazakhstan metals tycoon seeks two Hong Kong IPOs, as stock exchange looks to encourage overseas listings
  • DC League of Super-Pets movie review: Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart team up to save Superman in plot-heavy animated adventure
  • Do you have osteoarthritis in your knees and fear walking will cause pain? It actually strengthens the joints and lowers the risk of pain, study finds
  • She brought The Rolling Stones to Hong Kong: tributes flow for Colleen Ironside, music promoter
  • Is pregnancy glow just a myth? Why women can look better when expecting and skincare that can help if you’re not so lucky – expert advice
  • Sparkling tea is summer 2022’s drink of choice, not champagne – it’s perfect for picnics and some even promise skin benefits
  • K-drama midseason recap: Alchemy of Souls – Netflix fantasy drama starring Jung So-min mixes fast action with long pauses
  • Hong Kong’s social climbers befriend Instagram influencers today, before they employed different tactics – they make tempting targets for humour
  • Hong Kong film director Michael Hui on 30 years of writer’s block – ‘It’s like I’m trying to build a nuclear bomb’ – and why he has no regrets
  • Van Ness Wu on how new album Take a Ride marks his US homecoming and why the time is right as Asian representation in the US hits new heights
  • Meet rapper Eminem’s daughter, Hailie Jade Mathers – singing on her dad’s music earned her the Guinness World Record for youngest R&B chart entrant and now she hosts her own podcast
  • Meet Ann-Margret Olsson, Elvis Presley’s not-so-secret girlfriend: the Swedish Hollywood star played The King’s wife in Viva Las Vegas – but real-life fiancée Priscilla rightly had doubts
  • Who is Nina Agdal, Logan Paul’s rumoured girlfriend and Leonardo DiCaprio’s ex? The Danish bombshell modelled for Victoria’s Secret and once starred in a TV series with Kendall Jenner
  • 9 luxury hotels owned by celebrities: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi run hospitality groups, as does Robert De Niro with Nobu – and Leonardo DiCaprio’s private island resort is also in the works
  • Who is Nicole Shanahan, Elon Musk’s alleged hook-up and Sergey Brin’s wife? The lawyer and fellow tech whizz is divorcing Google’s co-founder, but was accused of having an affair – which Musk denied
  • How to start a whisky collection: rare and vintage bottles from The Macallan, Bowmore and Gordon & Macphail can be worth millions at auction, but remember to invest in what you like to drink too
  • Meet Princess Kako, Princess Mako’s younger sister: the 27-year-old Japanese royal is a fashionable figure skating champ – and a ‘symbol of hope’
  • Why are homebuyers ditching the British countryside for the city? Liverpool is a post-pandemic hotspot outpacing London, with Leeds, Manchester, York and Bristol properties on the rise too
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
